phases.title: Voter Registration
phases.description: The initial phase where citizens enroll in the national voter database.
phases.why: Registration ensures that only eligible citizens participate and prevents double voting.

eligibility: 18 years or older on election day

steps.title: Obtain Form A
steps.desc: Download or pick up the registration form from a local office.

steps.title: Submit Documentation
steps.desc: Submit the completed form along with required ID and address proofs.

phases.title: Voter List Verification
phases.description: Reviewing and correcting the preliminary voter list.
phases.why: Verification allows citizens to correct errors in their records before the final roll is published.

eligibility: Must have completed the Registration phase

steps.title: Check Preliminary List
steps.desc: Visit the online portal or local center to verify your entry.

steps.title: File Corrections
steps.desc: Submit a correction form if your name or details are incorrect.

phases.title: Polling Day
phases.description: The final stage where citizens cast their ballots.
phases.why: Polling day is the execution of the democratic mandate.

steps.title: Locate Station
steps.desc: Find your assigned polling booth using the station locator.

steps.title: Cast Vote
steps.desc: Present ID, get inked, and cast your ballot in the machine.

phases.title: Results and Tabulation
phases.description: The counting of votes and announcement of outcomes.
phases.why: Tabulation ensures the transparency and legitimacy of the election outcome.

steps.title: Real-time Monitoring
steps.desc: Follow official broadcast channels for live counting updates.

steps.title: Final Certification
steps.desc: Review the certified results published by the Election Commission.
